candidates.stmt:
Julie worked for Pearson Education for 18 years as part of the Rights and Permissions Legal Team as well as for the Contracts Dept. She was excellent in all areas of her job, wonderfully organised, and responsible for many new initiatives and process.  Julie was also passionate about driving on Environmental issues in the company and her local town.  She is a determined and dedicatd person and will be an excellent representative of The Green Party on Harlow Town Council Carolyn Tabor, Director Pearson Education

I would like to engage with the Community at Toddbrook and drive forward solutions to issues that the ward faces.  I have campaigned strongly against the proposed crossing over the River Stort over the last year and have a deep desire to protect our environment.  This is not something new to me having campaigned for Greenpeace several years ago and volunteered with the local conservationist group, including sitting on an invironmental forum at Harlow Council.  I have a good understanding of  many local issues environmental and social.

I believe in fairer, greener, safer communities.

If you would like a Councillor who will speak out and scrutinise council decisions- vote for me.

If you would like a councillor who will act as an independent voice in council vote- for me.

I will always put the needs of the residents of Old Harlow first. whether that be housing issues, car parking, children's play areas or pollution  etc.

I want to forge strong connections with the young and old of Old Harlow and support their needs.

I will fight to protect our green spaces, our children's play areas our parks and our woodlands.

I know that there are people suffering the effects of the cost of living in Harlow. I've been running the Freebies page for 15years now and see the hardship asks on there.

If you want someone who isn't afraid to challenge the council and speak- vote for me.

I'm delighted to be standing for re-election as your Labour & Co-operative candidate in Passmores ward! I am seeking your support to re-elect me on Thursday the 7th of May as your local voice to represent you on Harlow Council.

In the two year term I served I've resolved various casework issues across the ward and made several contributions in the Council chamber as your elected representative. I've ensured I've worked to represent all residents in our ward, irrespective of who you voted for or whether you voted at all - that's my pledge to you - to work hard, for each and every resident.

Promoted by Stefan Mullard-Toal on behalf of Jake Shepherd, both at Unit 1, Whitehall Estate, Flex Meadow, Harlow, CM19 5TP.

I’m Stephanie Marsh – Local Community Champion & your candidate in the upcoming local election on May 7th.

I’ve worked as a Taxi driver locally for over 30 years, & for some while a part time carer too. Driving a taxi gives me an “ear on the ground” as to local issues that are affecting Harlow residents.

The upcoming election is between the Greens & Reform in Passmores.
Across the country, support for the old parties is dropping away whilst current Yougov polling shows the Greens & Reform surging in popularity.

Reforms campaigns focus largely on Nigel Farage & national issues like crime, immigration & Tax. Harlow Council is responsible for bin collections, planning permissions, environmental health, parks & leisure, & grass cutting etc.

The person you elect will only have an impact on local issues.

Labour are losing more & more support every day!
Time & again Labour has failed to stand up for the people who needed them most.
By abandoning communities & breaking their promises, they’ve created a vacuum in our politics that Nigel Farage & Reform are now rushing to fill with empty slogans & division.

I can win in Passmores.

Dan Swords is telling anyone who will listen to let him finish the job, the conservatives, currently have a majority of one on the council. Which in turn means they can ride roughshod over any differing view of their vision of how the town should look. By electing myself as your Green Councilor, his majority evaporates, this doesn’t throw everything in jeopardy, as Mr. Swords would have you believe. He will have to consider & work with other points of view & listen to the concerns the residents of Passmores may have as to the direction of travel.

Please consider voting for me.

Best Wishes
Stephanie

A Message to the Electorate from Julie Bull
Subject: A Proven Voice for Sumners & Kingsmoor—Action, Not Broken Promises

Dear Neighbours,
My name is Julie Bull, and I am your Green Party candidate for Sumners & Kingsmoor in the upcoming local election on Thursday, 7th May 2026.

A councillor should be a tireless advocate for their community every day of the year. My track record shows I don’t just talk about local issues—I take direct action. From petitioning for zebra crossing improvements outside Milwards Primary School to my ongoing work monitoring the 23,000 proposed HGGT homes, I am committed to standing up for you.

My Priorities for Our Community:
Protecting Our Landscape & Services: I have been on the ground, attending meetings regarding the 122-acre solar farm proposal and the development of land at Jack’s Hatch. I have spoken directly with residents to understand your concerns about land use. While we need renewable energy and housing, it must be the right kind, in the right place, with the right infrastructure.

Healthcare Before Housing: I have submitted formal written objections against the Latton Priory development due to its glaring lack of healthcare provision. We cannot allow 1,300+ new homes without dedicated, on-site GP and dental services.

Holding Others to Account: In 2019, both the Conservatives and Labour promised a new hospital for Harlow. Seven years later, we are still waiting. I will continue to lobby for this promise to be kept while fighting for better access to GPs and a reduction in hospital waiting times today.

Fixing Our Roads & Infrastructure: I will demand an end to overrunning roadworks that cause daily gridlock and "patch-up" pothole repairs that fail within weeks. We need long-term, high-quality resurfacing and strict accountability for contractors who delay our journeys.

Integrity in Politics: As your councillor, I will strictly follow the Nolan Principles of Public Life—ensuring honesty, openness, and accountability remain
